first_paragraph: **[Snowboarding](/wiki/Snowboarding_at_the_Winter_Olympics "Snowboarding at the Winter Olympics") at the [2018 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2018_Winter_Olympics "2018 Winter Olympics")** was held at the [Bokwang Phoenix Park](/wiki/Phoenix_Pyeongchang "Phoenix Pyeongchang") and [Alpensia Ski Jumping Stadium](/wiki/Alpensia_Ski_Jumping_Stadium "Alpensia Ski Jumping Stadium") in [Pyeongchang](/wiki/Pyeongchang_County "Pyeongchang County"), [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea"). A total of ten snowboarding events were scheduled to take place between 10 and 24 February 2018.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

**[Snowboarding](/wiki/Snowboarding_at_the_Winter_Olympics "Snowboarding at the Winter Olympics") at the [2018 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2018_Winter_Olympics "2018 Winter Olympics")** was held at the [Bokwang Phoenix Park](/wiki/Phoenix_Pyeongchang "Phoenix Pyeongchang") and [Alpensia Ski Jumping Stadium](/wiki/Alpensia_Ski_Jumping_Stadium "Alpensia Ski Jumping Stadium") in [Pyeongchang](/wiki/Pyeongchang_County "Pyeongchang County"), [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea"). A total of ten snowboarding events were scheduled to take place between 10 and 24 February 2018.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The parallel slalom event, which made its debut four years earlier at the [2014 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2014_Winter_Olympics "2014 Winter Olympics") in Sochi, was dropped from the games in 2018 in favour of a new [big air](/wiki/Big_air "Big air") event.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Qualification

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Snowboarding_at_the_2018_Winter_Olympics&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Qualification")]

Main article: [Snowboarding at the 2018 Winter Olympics – Qualification](/wiki/Snowboarding_at_the_2018_Winter_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Qualification "Snowboarding at the 2018 Winter Olympics – Qualification")

A total of 258 quota spots were available to athletes to compete at the games (142 men and 116 women). Each [National Olympic Committee](/wiki/National_Olympic_Committee "National Olympic Committee") was permitted to enter a maximum of 26 athletes, with a maximum of 14 men or 14 women. Each event had its own maximum number of quota spots, however slopestyle and big air were calculated jointly per nation.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
